facts.text: Monmouth Junction sprang to life as the meeting point of three rail lines: the Pennsylvania Railroad’s New York division, Rocky Hill, and the Jamesburg‑Freehold branch.
Nestled in southwestern Middlesex County, Monmouth Junction sits just west of the county seat, New Brunswick, and 17 miles southeast of Trenton.
The community’s western edge is marked by a short stretch of Route 1, a historic corridor that once carried early travelers across New Jersey.
Covering 4.38 square miles, the CDP is almost entirely land, with only 0.57% water—just a sliver of small streams.
Water from Monmouth Junction drains southwest into Heathcote Brook and Devils Brook, both tributaries of the Millstone River in the Raritan watershed.
Monmouth Junction’s borders touch Heathcote to the northwest, Deans to the northeast, Dayton to the east, and Plainsboro Township to the southwest.
